MEL.name: Melanoma
MEL.description: A malignant skin cancer arising from melanocytes.
MEL.suggested_action: Consult a dermatologist promptly for professional evaluation and possible biopsy.

NV.name: Melanocytic Nevus
NV.description: A benign proliferation of melanocytes (commonly known as a mole).
NV.suggested_action: Routine monitoring. Consult a dermatologist if it changes in size, shape, or color.

BCC.name: Basal Cell Carcinoma
BCC.description: The most common form of skin cancer, arising from the basal cells of the epidermis.
BCC.suggested_action: Consult a dermatologist for evaluation and treatment options.

AKIEC.name: Actinic Keratoses and Intraepithelial Carcinoma
AKIEC.description: Pre-cancerous or early cancerous lesions caused by chronic sun exposure.
clinical_characteristics: Often on face, scalp, or hands
Can progress to squamous cell carcinoma
AKIEC.suggested_action: Consult a dermatologist for assessment. Preventative treatment may be required.

BKL.name: Benign Keratosis
BKL.description: A benign, non-cancerous skin growth that can appear wart-like or waxy.
BKL.suggested_action: Usually harmless. Consult a doctor if irritated, bleeding, or rapidly changing.

DF.name: Dermatofibroma
DF.description: A common benign fibrous nodule often occurring on the lower legs.
DF.suggested_action: Generally no treatment needed unless symptomatic or changing.

VASC.name: Vascular Lesion
VASC.description: A benign skin lesion composed of blood vessels (e.g., hemangioma).
clinical_characteristics: Often present from birth or appearing later
VASC.suggested_action: Typically harmless. Seek medical advice if it bleeds frequently or grows rapidly.
